title: Why Did I Stop Losing Weight? (Plateau Causes and Fixes)
introduction: If you are asking, "why did I stop losing weight," you are likely in a normal fat-loss plateau phase. This guide explains the top reasons weight loss stalls and gives a practical plan to restart progress.

sections.heading: 📌 Why Weight Loss Plateaus Happen
sections.content:
| Plateau Cause | What Changes | How It Feels |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Metabolic adaptation | Lower body mass needs fewer calories | Same eating, slower results |
| Tracking drift | Uncounted calories increase over time | "I eat the same" but trend is flat |
| Lower NEAT | Less daily movement while dieting | Deficit shrinks silently |
| Water retention | Stress, sodium, cycle, training effects | Scale stuck despite fat loss |

Plateaus are common and usually fixable.

sections.heading: The 14-Day Plateau Audit
sections.content:
Run this exact audit:

1) Use the [TDEE calculator](/tools/tdee-calculator)
2) Reconfirm targets with the [calorie deficit calculator](/tools/calorie-deficit-calculator)
3) Track all meals with the [meal log calculator](/tools/meal-log-calculator)
4) Track steps and sleep
5) Compare 7-day averages

Related article: [why am i not losing weight in a calorie deficit](/blog/why-am-i-not-losing-weight-in-a-calorie-deficit).

sections.heading: How to Break a Weight Loss Plateau
sections.content:
Best sequence:

• Tighten logging first
• Increase daily steps by 1,500-2,500
• Keep protein high with the [protein calculator](/tools/protein-calculator)
• If needed, reduce intake by 100-150 calories

Text CTA: Use Eati to compare your planned vs actual calories every day so plateau causes become obvious fast.

midArticleCta: Track your next 14 days in Eati to find exactly why your weight loss plateau started.
conclusion: A weight loss plateau is usually a math and behavior issue, not a personal failure. Recalculate, re-measure, and adjust one variable at a time to restart steady fat loss.
metaTitle: Why Did I Stop Losing Weight? Plateau Fix Strategy
metaDescription: Weight loss plateau? Learn why progress stalls and how to break a plateau with calorie, activity, and tracking adjustments.
targetKeyword: why did i stop losing weight

faqs.question: How long should a weight-loss plateau last before I change my plan?
faqs.answer: If your 7-day average is flat for about 2-4 weeks despite consistent tracking, it is usually time to adjust calories, movement, or both.
